commit.message:
Use Copilot CLI as AI backend for PR reviewer

Rewrites ai_reviewer.py to use Copilot CLI (copilot -p) as the primary
AI backend, with Azure OpenAI as fallback. Writes PR diffs to a temp
file to avoid Windows command-line length limits, then gives Copilot
a short prompt pointing to the file.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
refactor(jetson): pre-build engines on startup + simplify config flow

- SyncEngine.run(): check desired model_versions vs loaded engines
  at startup, trigger builds immediately instead of waiting for
  first poll cycle (5min). Engines built in background thread.
- main.py: simplified startup config to single source (SyncEngine)
  instead of duplicate device_state read. Removed redundant block.

Config flow: Admin → Supabase → SyncEngine (single writer) →
  main.py reads via get_active_config()

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add Players MVP tab with rankings and detail pages

- New /api/players endpoint ranks 483 players by MVP Score (0-100)
  - Score uses 4 distinct signals: portfolio value (40%), icon card (25%),
    card breadth (20%), and rookie premium ratio (15%)
  - Includes confidence indicator (high/medium/low) based on real price data %
  - Market changes labeled as simulated
- New /api/players/{name} detail endpoint with trophy case, career timeline,
  set presence, comparable players, and auto-generated fun facts
- Frontend landing page with hero #1 player, sortable grid, search
- Frontend detail page with diamond radar chart for score breakdown,
  trophy case gallery, career timeline bar chart, full card portfolio table
- Added Players tab to nav between Top Cards and Teams

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: runtime color theme system (get_sys_color / set_sys_colors) (#89)

* feat: add runtime color theme system (get_sys_color / set_sys_colors)

- Add sys_color_idx_t enum (22 slots) and get_sys_color() macro to messages.h
- Keep legacy COLOR_* macros as default-value constants for backward compat
- Implement g_sys_colors[] default table and set_sys_colors() in new user/theme.c
- New slots: kColorActiveTitlebar, kColorActiveTitlebarText,
  kColorInactiveTitlebar, kColorInactiveTitlebarText (focused vs unfocused),
  kColorWorkspaceBg (darker canvas/document area), kColorFolderText
- draw_window_controls() now uses active/inactive titlebar color based on focus
- Title text drawn in active/inactive titlebar text color based on focus
- commctl/image.c workspace uses kColorWorkspaceBg (distinct from statusbar)
- commctl/filelist.c folder color moved into theme table (kColorFolderText)
- All COLOR_* usages in user/ and commctl/ replaced with get_sys_color()

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/corepunch/orion-ui/sessions/82a91edc-876f-4c9b-848c-aba83dae8dc3

Co-authored-by: corepunch <83646194+corepunch@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: use richer blue for active titlebar in dark theme

Update kColorActiveTitlebar from 0xff1a3a5a (very dark navy) to 0xff1e5aa0 (medium saturated blue) — visually distinct against the dark gray panels while staying suitable for a dark UI.

repository.name: orion-ui
repository.description: Orion is a retro-styled, cross-platform UI framework written in C. The framework is not embedded or mobile-targeted — it is a desktop GUI framework designed for Linux, macOS, and Windows. Its central design philosophy is to faithfully reproduce the Windows API (Win32) message-based programming model in a portable, hardware-accelerated context.

commit.message:
docs: 更新 README.md 补充硬件要求、测试命令和配置说明

- 新增硬件接口对照表（CAN/UART/GPIO 与设备的映射）
- 注明 unit_tests（无需真实硬件）vs hw_tests（需要真实硬件）
- 补充全部 hw_tests 硬件集成测试命令和安全须知
- 添加 RK3576 gpio.use_irq=false 必要性说明
- 添加关键配置项说明表格（含 gpio.use_irq、diagnostics.local_path）

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(jetson): apply admin config on startup + refresh engines each cycle

- Startup: load desired_config from device_state.json immediately
  (no need to wait for first poll cycle to apply admin settings)
- Main loop: refresh available_engines() each cycle to detect
  hot-swapped engines, fallback if current leaf_type removed
- Prediction log now includes model name + version:
  'Prediction: Late_blight (50.9%) [model=tomato v1.0.0]'

commit.message:
docs: 修正 API_REFERENCE.md 多处错误与过时描述

- 架构图 BMS(Modbus) → BMS(UART)
- Section 5.4 BMS：全面重写为嘉佰达 V4 UART 接口（ISerialPort 构造，
  修正 current_a 注释正=充电，更新 BatteryData/Diagnostics 字段，
  添加 BMS2 Modbus 未接线备注及符号差异说明）
- HealthService 模式对比表 current → torque_a
- 补充完整 JSONL 载荷示例和 torque_a vs bms.current 区别说明
- 删除陈旧 DiagnosticsCollector 迁移存根注意（类已不存在）
- 配置示例与 config.json 实际值对齐：
  ttyS8（BMS），gpiochip5/line0-1，IMU 9600 baud
  补充 gpio.use_irq 字段，添加关键字段说明表
- 线程表 bms 注释：Modbus → UART
- 同步语义表格 WalkMotor 描述（已为纯类型定义）
- 修正流程图中 Health 字段注释 current → torque_a
- 标记 16.15 LB/RB 问题为已修复（commit 6cb831c0）
- 修正驱动层构造示例：ttyS1 921600→9600，gpiochip0→gpiochip5
- 更新文档版本说明和日期

feat(render_tex): support Tuple inline Array in TeX rendering (P3-T04)

- Tuple branch in collect_lines now renders Array children inline as
  A_1 \ A_2 \ \cdots \ A_{C} instead of \texttt{<?>}
- Repeat body Tuple now handles both Scalar and Array children,
  rendering A_{k,1} \ A_{k,2} \ \cdots \ A_{k,C_k} per row
- Extracted render_tuple_line helper to stay within 100-line limit
- Added RepeatTupleElem enum for polymorphic row rendering

commit.message:
fix(jetson): find trtexec in known paths when not in PATH

trtexec on Jetson is typically at /usr/src/tensorrt/bin/trtexec
which is not in systemd service PATH. Now searches known locations
before falling back to error.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: レイアウトのナビリンク先を現行URLへ修正

Add user management interface for admins (#47)

* feat(F-01): lägg till användarhantering via UI

- Ny admin-sida /users: lista, bjud in och ta bort användare
- Supabase Edge Function (invite-user) hanterar invite/delete med
  service_role-nyckel – nyckeln exponeras aldrig i frontend
- Ny service web/src/services/users.ts med listUsers/inviteUser/deleteUser
- NavBar-länk "Användare" visas bara för admin
- Inbyggt skydd: admin kan inte ta bort sitt eget konto
- TDD: E2E-tester skrivna först i tests/e2e/app.spec.ts +
  seedDeletableUser/cleanupDeletableUser-helpers

https://claude.ai/code/session_019NJjHVpevD9pBx71ebQoav

* fix: lägg till action=list i Edge Function och åtgärda testbuggar

Rotorsaken till CI-felet: users.ts anropar action='list' men Edge
Function saknade hanteraren – returnerade 'Okänd åtgärd' → hela
/users-sidan misslyckades.

- Edge Function: lägg till action='list' (hämtar profiles med admin-klient)
- Edge Function: svenska delete-felmeddelanden (exponerar ej error.message)
- Users.tsx: lägg till loadError-state med role="alert" vid listningsfel
- app.spec.ts: fixa waitForResponse-predikat – kontrollera payload.success
  och payload.userId istf payload.user.email (som ej finns i svaret)

https://claude.ai/code/session_019NJjHVpevD9pBx71ebQoav

- deploy.yml: deploya invite-user efter frontend-deploy (kräver SUPABASE_ACCESS_TOKEN)
- deploy-edge-functions.yml: manuell workflow_dispatch för initial/ad-hoc deployment

SUPABASE_ACCESS_TOKEN måste läggas till som secret i "Supabase"-miljön.
Skaffa token: https://supabase.com/dashboard/account/tokens

commit.message:
feat(render): support Tuple inline Array in plain text rendering (P3-T03)

Extend the plain text input format renderer so that when a Tuple
contains an Array child, it renders the Array elements inline instead
of showing <?>.

- Top-level Tuple: N A_1 A_2 … A_C M
- Repeat body Tuple: N_i A_{i,1} A_{i,2} … A_{i,C_i} M_i

Extract render_tuple_elements helper to keep render_node under the
clippy too_many_lines threshold.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: add optional rate limiting support for features endpoint

Allow consumers to apply rate limiting to the /features/{featureName}
endpoint by passing a named policy string to UseFeaturesDashboard().

keeping the change fully backward-compatible.

fix: 重命名 JSONL 行走电机电流字段 current → torque_a

walk.*.current 存储的是 CAN 状态帧解码的 Q轴转矩相电流（±33A），
与 BMS 电池包总电流（bms.current）物理量不同，同名导致分析误判。

- HEALTH 模式：walk.current → walk.torque_a
- DIAGNOSTICS 模式：walk.lt/rt/lb/rb.current → walk.*.torque_a
- JSON 模板键同步更新，无功能逻辑变更

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(sample): support Tuple inline Array in output rendering (P3-T02)

Verified that the existing Tuple branches in emit_node and
emit_node_with_values already correctly handle Array children:
- Generator stores SampleValue::Array keyed by the Array NodeId
- Tuple emission iterates children and calls format_value on each
- format_value already handles SampleValue::Array (space-joined)

Added two E2E tests confirming the behavior:
- e2e_tuple_inline_array_in_repeat: Repeat(M) { Tuple(C, A[len=C], R) }
- e2e_tuple_inline_array_top_level: Tuple(N, A[len=N])

commit.message:
chore: 删除 API_REFERENCE.md.bak 备份残留

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Unified PaperMCP: 30+ academic paper sources in one MCP server

Merged PaperMCP and openags/paper-search-mcp into a single unified project.

Sources (30+):
- ArXiv, PubMed, Semantic Scholar, CrossRef, OpenAlex, DBLP
- Google Scholar (scholarly + HTML), bioRxiv, medRxiv, Europe PMC, PMC
- HuggingFace, PapersWithCode, OpenReview, HAL, DOAJ, Zenodo
- OpenAIRE, INSPIRE-HEP, Unpaywall, IACR, CORE, CiteSeerX
- SSRN, BASE, ChemRxiv, Sci-Hub, IEEE (skeleton), ACM (skeleton)

Features:
- 4 unified MCP tools: search, download, read, list_papers
- CLI interface: paper-search command for terminal usage
- Docker support, Smithery deployment config
- Parallel search across all sources via unified dispatcher

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: correct recursive call detection and remove redundant using

- Fix SemanticAnalyzer to compare call.FunctionName against the subject's
  function name (_currentFuncName) rather than the requirement ID
- Remove redundant 'using System.Linq' from StructType.cs (covered by ImplicitUsings)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: axperotto <17514558+axperotto@users.noreply.github.com>

perf: benchmark suite (15 categories, 33 benchmarks)

Compared against YamlDotNet and VYaml:
- POCO serialization/deserialization
- Block YAML serialization/deserialization
- DOM parsing
- Large document (200 entries)
- Anchor/alias resolution
- Kubernetes Deployment manifest
- Multi-document parsing
- Docker Compose with aliases
- GitHub Actions mixed flow/block
- Block scalars (ConfigMap)
- Helm values (deep nesting)
- Source-gen vs reflection

Key results vs YamlDotNet:
- Serialization: 15x faster
- Deserialization: 1.8-2.9x faster
- K8s manifest: 2.9x faster, 3.6x less memory

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
test: comprehensive test suite (85 tests, 267/267 conformance)

Unit tests:
- POCO round-trip serialization/deserialization
- Scalar types (bool, int, decimal, quoted strings)
- Block scalars (literal, folded, strip, keep, clip)
- Flow collections, anchors/aliases, merge keys
- Explicit keys, duplicate key rejection
- Schema modes (Core, Failsafe, Yaml11)
- Utf8YamlReader token stream tests

Real-world YAML tests:
- Kubernetes Deployment, Service, ConfigMap, CRD, multi-document
- Docker Compose with anchors/merge keys
- GitHub Actions workflow
- Helm values with deep nesting
- Edge cases: colons in values, special keys, numerics, comments, deep nesting

Conformance:
- yaml-test-suite integration (267/267 load cases = 100%)
- Curated corpus with 11 categories

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: reader, writer, and supporting types

- Utf8YamlReader — forward-only token stream reader
- Utf8YamlWriter — block/flow YAML output writer
- YamlReaderOptions — schema, depth limits, alias limits
- YamlWriterOptions — indentation, newline, document markers
- YamlSchema — Core, Json, Failsafe, Yaml11
- YamlDuplicateKeyHandling — Allow/Disallow
- YamlException — YAML-specific error type

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: system.text.yaml
repository.description: High-performance YAML 1.2 library for .NET. Zero dependencies. 267/267 yaml-test-suite conformance.

commit.message:
feat: native POCO serializer and deserializer

YamlSerializer — public API:
- Serialize<T>/Deserialize<T> with YamlSerializerOptions
- DeserializeAll for multi-document streams
- UTF-8 byte overloads

YamlObjectMapper — reflection-based deserializer:
- Type-cached property discovery
- [YamlPropertyName]/[YamlIgnore] attributes
- Scalar→CLR conversion (string/bool/int/long/float/double/decimal/DateTime/Guid/enum)
- Collection mapping (T[], List<T>, Dictionary<K,V>)
- YamlNamingPolicy (PascalCase/camelCase/snake_case/kebab-case)
- YAML 1.1 legacy boolean support

YamlObjectWriter — direct POCO→YAML serializer:
- Block-style output with configurable indentation
- Multiline strings as literal block scalars (|)
- Smart quoting for special values

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: system.text.yaml
repository.description: High-performance YAML 1.2 library for .NET. Zero dependencies. 267/267 yaml-test-suite conformance.

commit.message:
feat: native YAML DOM and composer

YamlNode hierarchy (zero System.Text.Json dependency):
- YamlScalarNode — values with style tracking
- YamlSequenceNode — ordered children, IEnumerable<YamlNode?>
- YamlMappingNode — ordered KV pairs with complex key support
- Convenience navigation: indexers, GetScalarValue/Int/Long/Bool

YamlNodeComposer — scanner tokens to YamlNode tree:
- Anchor/alias resolution with clone-on-alias
- Merge key (<<) support
- Tag directive processing (%YAML, %TAG)
- Duplicate key detection
- Block/flow sequence and mapping composition

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: system.text.yaml
repository.description: High-performance YAML 1.2 library for .NET. Zero dependencies. 267/267 yaml-test-suite conformance.

commit.message:
feat: character-level YAML 1.2 scanner

Utf8YamlScanner implements a libyaml-inspired character-level tokenizer:
- Simple-key tracking with indent stack
- Flow/block context separation
- Block scalar scanning (literal |, folded >)
- Double/single quoted scalar scanning with escape sequences
- Plain scalar scanning with document indicator detection
- Anchor, alias, and tag token recognition
- AggressiveInlining on hot-path methods
- Pooled StringBuilders for zero per-scalar allocation

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: add Ctrl+J as universal newline key for multiline input

Most macOS terminals (Terminal.app, iTerm2) send identical \r for both
Enter and Shift+Enter — there's no way to distinguish them. Only Kitty
and WezTerm support the Kitty keyboard protocol (\x1b[13;2u).

Fix: Add Ctrl+J (\n / LF) as cross-terminal newline. In raw mode, Enter
sends \r (CR) while Ctrl+J sends \n (LF), making them distinguishable
on ALL terminal emulators.

Newline options:
- Ctrl+J: Works everywhere (recommended)
- Shift+Enter: Works on Kitty/WezTerm (Kitty protocol)
- Alt+Enter: Works on iTerm2 and some terminals
- Backslash + Enter: Always works (manual continuation)

Also: added multiline hint to banner UI.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
perf: avoid HashSet allocation in role requirement handlers (#221)

Replace Intersect().Any() with Any(Contains) in RealmAccessRequirementHandler
and ResourceAccessRequirementHandler. The Enumerable.Intersect() method
internally allocates a HashSet on every call, which is unnecessary overhead
in the hot authorization path (called once per authenticated request per
protected endpoint).

For typical role lists (1-10 items), direct Contains checks are faster
due to lower allocation pressure and better cache locality.

Co-authored-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: fetch actual card images via DuckDuckGo + Bing (no API keys)

- scripts/fetch_card_images.py — searches DDG and Bing for real card photos
  - Scores results by domain reliability (Heritage Auctions, PSA, Beckett preferred)
  - Exponential backoff on DDG rate limits, automatic Bing fallback
  - Graceful shutdown (SIGINT/SIGTERM), incremental DB saves
  - 100% hit rate on first 95 cards tested
- Fix: /top endpoint now includes image_url in response (was missing)

Usage:
  python3 scripts/fetch_card_images.py              # Fetch all missing
  python3 scripts/fetch_card_images.py --progress   # Show stats
  python3 scripts/fetch_card_images.py --limit 100  # First 100 only

perf: single-pass org claims scan and avoid ToArray in VerificationPlan (#238)

- KeycloakOrganizationClaimsExtensions.GetOrganizations: replace the
  three-step Where→ToList / FirstOrDefault / Where→Select→ToList pattern
  with a single foreach over the claims collection. The JSON claim is
  detected eagerly and short-circuits the loop; string aliases are
  lazily accumulated in a List<string>? to avoid allocation when there
  are no matching claims at all.

- VerificationPlan: change the internal scopes store from
  Dictionary<string,List<string>> to Dictionary<string,string[]>. This
  lets GetEnumerator() pass the stored array directly to
  ProtectedResourceAttribute instead of calling .ToArray() on every
  iteration, eliminating one heap allocation per resource per
  enumeration. The Add() path handles the rare multi-scope case with a
  simple array copy.

Co-authored-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: correct base href for local dev and fix GH Pages deploy workflow

- Change <base href> from '/blazor-webassembly/' to '/' in index.html
  so local dotnet run works without CSS/JS 404 errors.
  The '#blazor-error-ui' div was always visible because app.css
  (which hides it with display:none) was failing to load.
- Update deploy.yml to patch base href back to '/blazor-webassembly/'
  during publish using sed, so GitHub Pages deployment still works.
- Fix missing actions/upload-pages-artifact step in deploy workflow
  (without it, the deploy job had nothing to deploy).
